subject: Berkshire Hathaway Operating Subsidiaries — Avatar 7 of 8 Complete
message:
Berkshire Hathaway operating subsidiaries research complete. Avatar 7 of 8.

**Output file:** `.library/custom/buffett-operating/SKILL.md`
Full path: `/Users/huangzesen/work/lingtai-projects/impersonate/buffet/impersonate-meta/.lingtai/operating-businesses/.library/custom/buffett-operating/SKILL.md`

**Sections covered:**
1. BNSF Railway — acquisition 2009 ($44B including debt), railroad economics, CEO succession (Rose → Ice)
2. Berkshire Hathaway Energy — MidAmerican/NV Energy/Pacific Power, the "business I'd build from scratch" quote, Greg Abel connection
3. See's Candy — 1972 acquisition, pricing power model, $25M → $2B+ earnings story
4. Dairy Queen — 1997 acquisition, 7,000+ stores, Blizzard brand
5. Pampered Chef — 2002 acquisition, direct-sales model, Carl Ice connection
6. Shaw Industries — 2001 flooring acquisition, world's largest carpet maker
7. MiTek — building products, metal connectors, 2001
8. Lubrizol — 2011 $9.7B specialty chemicals acquisition from BP
9. ISCAR — 2006 $5B Israeli metalworking tools
10. Precision Castparts — 2016 $37B aerospace acquisition, 2020 sale at ~$12B loss, Buffett's admission
11. Nebraska Furniture Mart — Rose Blumkin ("Mrs. B") story, 1983 handshake deal, expansion to Kansas/Colorado/Texas
12. Borsheims — 1989 jewelry, Solly Borsheims handshake deal
13. Clayton Homes — 2008 controversy, subprime lending, Buffett's 2008 admission
14. NetJets — 1998 fractional jet ownership, fleet of 700+ aircraft
15. XTRA Lease — trailer leasing
16. Acquisition philosophy — five criteria, "wonderful businesses at fair prices"
17. Autonomy model — no budgets, no interference, centralized capital allocation
18. Notable failures — Dexter Shoes, Precision Castparts, RC2
19. Permanent holdings doctrine — "favorite holding period is forever"

All claims are sourced or marked [unverified]. No fabricated anecdotes footer included.
